content.full_text:
SUE C. HESTOR
Attorney at Law
870 Market Street, Suite 1121
San Francisco, CA 94102
(415) 362-2778


June 18, 1992

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento CA 95814

To Whom it May Concern:

I am the attorney for Susan Bierman, candidate for the San Francisco Board of Supervisors and need assistance in understanding the application of Government Code 84308(b) and (d) to particular situations.

Ms. Bierman was a San Francisco City Planning Commissioner for sixteen years. She was removed by the current mayor on March 17, 1992 and is a candidate for office in November 1992. Three former Planning Commissioners, who were also removed, are active in Ms. Bierman's campaign, two of them on her Finance Committee.

I have reviewed the agendas and minutes for all matters that came before the Planning Commission from January 1 - March 17, 1992 to determine who testified on every matter before the Commission. I have provided to be to persons soliciting funds on Ms. Bierman's behalf, her treasurer, and to Ms. Bierman herself a memo setting out what I believe the conflict provisions in §84308. In an abundance of caution I have proscribed any solicitation of funds, in any amount, from persons who appeared on any matter until at least four months after the matter became final, although the statutory limit only affects contributions over $250 for a period of three months. Unless we have clear evidence to the contrary, we consider all persons who appeared to meet the statutory standard of being either a "party" or a "participant" with a financial interest in the decision.

My problem is with the following language of § 84308(b) and (c): "and for three months following the date a final decision is rendered by the agency in the proceeding." This relates back to "a proceeding before an agency involving a license, permit, or other entitlement." Here are the specific fact situations where my client would like guidance:

13

June 18, 1992 - FPPC - page 2

(a) Natoma Mews - The only proceeding before the former
Planning Commission and Ms. Bierman was a hearing taking
testimony on the Draft EIR. No action was taken by the
Planning Commission on either the EIR or any entitlement
for the project. The project itself and its permit will
be heard by the newly appointed Planning Commission
sometime later this year. Was a hearing on the Draft EIR
on the project a "proceeding involving a license, permit
or other entitlement for use." Is my client's campaign
barred from accepting contributions from the attorneys in
the law firm representing the developer of the project,
even though Ms. Bierman will have no role in the approval
of the project, and even though that approval will occur
many months after Ms. Bierman has left the Planning
Commission? Does the conflict as to that firm apply to
all members of that firm, including those who do not
represent Natoma Mews? To all associates who are not
partners, even to those who do not represent Natoma
Mews?

(b) There is a similar situation with regard to a Costco
project. The Draft EIR was heard by the old Planning
Commission on January 16, 1992. The EIR was certified
and the project approved by the new Planning Commission
on April 16, 1992. Is the bar on accepting contributions
determined by the date the new Planning Commission acted
on the project?

(c) San Francisco Public Library - The former Planning
Commission and Ms. Bierman voted to certify the EIR on
the project on February 27, 1992. The new Planning
Commission decided to not review the project and ceased
its jurisdiction on May 7, 1992. As to anyone with a
financial stake in the project (e.g. consultants), is Ms.
Bierman's campaign prohibited from accepting a
contribution until 3 months after February 27 (when Ms.
Bierman voted on the project), March 17 (when Ms. Bierman
was removed from the Planning Commission), or May 7, 1992
(when the new Planning Commission decided to take no
action)? Since this is a public project, my construction
is the Library Commission's, and staff are not
constrained by the limits in §84308 and may make whatever
contribution (up to the San Francisco local limit of
$500) they feel appropriate. Also, many members of the
public testified on one side or the other of this
project. Since they have no financial stake in the
project, they may similarly make a contribution. Do you
concur?

14

June 18, 1992 - FPPC - page 3

(d)  A similar situation exists as to the expansion of the San
Francisco Airport. Hearings on the Draft EIR were
conducted by the old Planning Commission. The new
Planning Commission certified the EIR and made Master
Plan findings on May 28, 1992. From what date is the
three month period calculated?

(e)  Several projects had hearings on the merits by both the
old and new Planning Commissions. The actions on them
were taken solely by the new Commission. Is the
starting date for calculating the three months the date
of the last hearing by the old Planning Commission, the
date Ms. Bierman was removed from the Planning
Commission, or the date the new Planning Commission
approved (or disapproved) the project.

I believe this is the universe of questions I have at this
time. I have instructed the campaign to accept no
contributions in any amount from anyone with a possible
financial interest in any of the above described projects
until and unless your office responds in writing. If you have
any questions about this matter, please don't hesitate to call
me.

Sincerely,

Sue C. Hestor

cc:  Sue Bierman
     Doug Engmann, Finance Chair
     Eileen Adams, Treasurer


15
